docs.title: ZEN API
docs.text: Python wrappers for the ZEN API, enabling programmatic control of ZEISS microscopy software via gRPC.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
This package is not available on PyPI. Install from the wheel file:

pip install zen_api-<version>-py3-none-any.whl

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python >= 3.10
  • betterproto == 2.0.0b7

docs.title: API Domains
docs.text:
The ZEN API is organized into several domains:

Domain Description acquisition Experiment and image acquisition services application Application-level composition services common Shared data types and enumerations em Electron microscopy hardware and workflow services hardware Microscope hardware control (stages, axes) lm Light microscopy acquisition, hardware, live scan, and slide scan services workflows Automated workflow and job management services

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from zen_api.acquisition.v1beta import ExperimentServiceStub

# Connect to ZEN API server
# (requires ZEN software running with API server enabled)

docs.title: PixelType
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Pixel type of image data.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default value if status is not specified.

  • GRAY8 –

    8 bit unsigned.

  • GRAY16 –

    16 bit unsigned.

  • BGR24 –

    8 bit triples, representing the color channels Blue, Green and Red.

  • BGR48 –

    16 bit triples, representing the color channels Blue, Green and Red.

docs.text:

docs.title: ExperimentStatus dataclass
docs.text:
ExperimentStatus(
    tiles_index: int = betterproto.int32_field(1),
    tiles_count: int = betterproto.int32_field(2),
    cumulated_tiles_count: int = betterproto.int32_field(3),
    scenes_index: int = betterproto.int32_field(4),
    scenes_count: int = betterproto.int32_field(5),
    time_points_index: int = betterproto.int32_field(6),
    time_points_count: int = betterproto.int32_field(7),
    zstack_slices_index: int = betterproto.int32_field(8),
    zstack_slices_count: int = betterproto.int32_field(9),
    channels_index: int = betterproto.int32_field(10),
    channels_count: int = betterproto.int32_field(11),
    images_acquired_index: int = betterproto.int32_field(
        12
    ),
    images_count: int = betterproto.int32_field(13),
    is_experiment_running: bool = betterproto.bool_field(
        14
    ),
    is_acquisition_running: bool = betterproto.bool_field(
        15
    ),
    total_elapsed_time: timedelta = betterproto.message_field(
        16
    ),
)

Bases: Message

Status of an experiment. The index properties are 0-based. If a value is 0 or -1 it means the value is not initialized or the running experiment does not have the corresponding dimension. In case of indices, 0 value can also refer to the first element.

Attributes:

  • tiles_index (int) –

    The current or already acquired (depending of the dimension order) tiles position index

  • tiles_count (int) –

    The tiles count of the currently acquired scene.

  • cumulated_tiles_count (int) –

    The total tiles count of all scenes.

  • scenes_index (int) –

    The current or already acquired (depending of the dimension order) scene index (= tile region/position index).

  • scenes_count (int) –

    The total scene count (= tile region/position count).

  • time_points_index (int) –

    The current or already acquired (depending of the dimension order) time point index in time series.

  • time_points_count (int) –

    The number of time points in time series.

  • zstack_slices_index (int) –

    The current or already acquired (depending of the dimension order) z-stack slice index.

  • zstack_slices_count (int) –

    The total count of z-stack slices.

  • channels_index (int) –

    The current or already acquired (depending of the dimension order) channel index.

  • channels_count (int) –

    The total channel count.

  • images_acquired_index (int) –

    The number of acquired images over all dimensions (channels, time series, z-stack, cumulated tiles).

  • images_count (int) –

    The images count over all dimensions (channels, time series, z-stack, cumulated tiles).

  • is_experiment_running (bool) –

    A value indicating whether the experiment is currently running.

  • is_acquisition_running (bool) –

    A value indicating whether an acquisition is currently running.

  • total_elapsed_time (timedelta) –

    The total time that elapsed since the start of the running experiment.

docs.title: experiment_id class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
experiment_id: str = string_field(1)

ID of an experiment for which status is requested. If ID is provided, the status can be retrieved for both active and finished experiments. If ID is not provided, status of one of the active experiments is returned. If ID is not provided and there are no active experiments, an exception is thrown.

docs.title: FramePixelData dataclass
docs.text:
FramePixelData(
    start_position: IntPoint = betterproto.message_field(1),
    size: IntSize = betterproto.message_field(2),
    pixel_type: PixelType = betterproto.enum_field(3),
    raw_data: bytes = betterproto.bytes_field(4),
)

Bases: Message

A simple container for the frame pixel data. This can contain either the pixels of a full frame or only a part of it (e.g., a line or a rectangle in case of partial acquisition when working with EM and LSM).

Attributes:

  • start_position (IntPoint) –

    The start position (in pixels) of the pixel data in the frame.

  • size (IntSize) –

    Size (in pixels) of the pixel data.

  • pixel_type (PixelType) –

    The pixel type of the frame.

  • raw_data (bytes) –

    The raw pixel data.

docs.title: start_position class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
start_position: IntPoint = message_field(1)

The start position (in pixels) of the pixel data in the frame. Together with the size property, this represents the rectangle where the pixels are located inside the full frame. For ordinary acquisition this will be the top left corner of the frame, but for partial acquisition this can be any position inside the full frame relative to the top left corner.

docs.title: size class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
size: IntSize = message_field(2)

Size (in pixels) of the pixel data. Together with the start position property, this represents the rectangle where the pixels are located inside the full frame. For ordinary acquisition this will be the full frame size, but for partial acquisition this can be just one part of the full frame.

docs.title: pixel_type class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
pixel_type: PixelType = enum_field(3)

The pixel type of the frame.

docs.title: raw_data class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
raw_data: bytes = bytes_field(4)

The raw pixel data. The value of individual pixels is contained in this container. The pixel values need to be extracted from the raw byte data. The number of bits needed to extract a single pixel from the raw byte data and the format it is stored in is determined by the pixel type property.

docs.title: channel_index class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
channel_index: Optional[int] = message_field(
    1, wraps=TYPE_INT32
)

Optional parameter for filtering by specific channel. If the channel index is provided, then only that channel will be monitored, but if the channel index is not provided, then all channels will be monitored.

docs.title: enable_raw_data class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
enable_raw_data: bool = bool_field(2)

Value indicating whether the streamed frame data contains raw frames as received from the acquisition (when set), which can be either full or partial frames (e.g., in general LM cameras produce full frames and LSM and EM detectors produce partial frames/lines). Otherwise (when not set) the streamed frame data contains only full frames, which means that in the case of partial frames/lines, they would be assembled into full frames.

docs.title: AcquisitionResponseType
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Indicates the type of response returned by the camera live image acquisition stream.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Unspecified type.

  • FRAME_START –

    The response is the first data buffer of a new frame. The response will include the image meta-data.

  • FRAME_CONTINUATION –

    The response is a continuation of the previous frame. The response will not include the image meta-data.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Unspecified type.

docs.title: FRAME_START class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
FRAME_START = 1

The response is the first data buffer of a new frame. The response will include the image meta-data.

docs.title: FRAME_CONTINUATION class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
FRAME_CONTINUATION = 2

The response is a continuation of the previous frame. The response will not include the image meta-data.

docs.title: AcquisitionStatus
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Acquisition states.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • IDLE –

    No acquisition task is running, available for next acquisition.

  • BUSY –

    Acquisition hardware is occupied by another task.

  • LIVE_RUNNING –

    Live acquisition is currently running.

  • ACQUISITION_RUNNING –

    Acquisition task is currently running.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: IDLE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
IDLE = 1

No acquisition task is running, available for next acquisition.

docs.title: BUSY class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
BUSY = 2

Acquisition hardware is occupied by another task. No acquisition possible at the moment.

docs.title: LIVE_RUNNING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
LIVE_RUNNING = 3

Live acquisition is currently running.

docs.title: ACQUISITION_RUNNING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
ACQUISITION_RUNNING = 4

Acquisition task is currently running.

docs.title: BeamState
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Possible beam operating states.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • UNKNOWN –

    Unknown indicates that this variable hasn't been assigned yet.

  • ERROR –

    There is an error with the Gun or EHT and the beam cannot be used.

  • OFF –

    EHT is off.

  • TURNING_ON –

    The EHT (and gun for C-SEM) is turning on.

  • ON –

    Gun is on.

  • GOING_TO_STANDBY –

    The beam is going into the standby state.

  • STANDBY –

    Not all devices support this mode.

  • TURNING_OFF –

    The EHT is ramping down.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: UNKNOWN class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNKNOWN = 1

Unknown indicates that this variable hasn't been assigned yet. This value should never occur. If you read this value, it's a bug.

docs.title: ERROR class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
ERROR = 2

There is an error with the Gun or EHT and the beam cannot be used.

docs.title: OFF class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
OFF = 3

EHT is off. On FE-SEMs, the gun may be on, gun interfaces must be checked to discover gun state. On C-SEMs the EHT and gun are off, and there is no current on the filament.

docs.title: TURNING_ON class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
TURNING_ON = 4

The EHT (and gun for C-SEM) is turning on.

docs.title: ON class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
ON = 5

Gun is on. Filament (if it exists) has full current. EHT is on. The beam can be used.

docs.title: GOING_TO_STANDBY class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
GOING_TO_STANDBY = 6

The beam is going into the standby state.

docs.title: STANDBY class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
STANDBY = 7

Not all devices support this mode. This represents an intermediate state. The EVO and the FIB have standby states.

docs.title: TURNING_OFF class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
TURNING_OFF = 8

The EHT is ramping down. On C-SEM, the gun and filament may also be shutting down.

docs.title: CameraAcquisitionStatus
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Acquisition states.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • UNKNOWN –

    Should not occur in an initialized system.

  • ERROR –

    The image acquisition device is in an error state and cannot be used.

  • IDLE –

    The image acquisition device is ready to perform a task.

  • BUSY –

    The image acquisition device is busy with other tasks and cannot be used for image acquisition.

  • LIVE_ACQUISITION –

    The system is performing a scan and will continue until explicitly asked to stop.

  • SINGLE_ACQUISITION –

    The system is performing a scan that has a scheduled

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: UNKNOWN class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNKNOWN = 1

Should not occur in an initialized system.

docs.title: ERROR class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
ERROR = 2

The image acquisition device is in an error state and cannot be used.

docs.title: IDLE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
IDLE = 3

The image acquisition device is ready to perform a task.

docs.title: BUSY class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
BUSY = 4

The image acquisition device is busy with other tasks and cannot be used for image acquisition.

docs.title: LIVE_ACQUISITION class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
LIVE_ACQUISITION = 5

The system is performing a scan and will continue until explicitly asked to stop. We use the term "Live" instead of "Continuous" because in ZIS, "Continuous" means "a time series of snaps with no delay between and no scheduled end".

docs.title: SINGLE_ACQUISITION class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
SINGLE_ACQUISITION = 6

The system is performing a scan that has a scheduled termination such as "at the end of the frame".

docs.title: CcdMode
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

The CCD mode enumeration.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • OFF –

    Illumination is off.

  • AUTO –

    Illumination mode is determined based on detector selection.

  • GREYSCALE –

    Illumination is Infra-red, producing greyscale images.

  • COLOR –

    Illumination is White light, producing color images.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: OFF class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
OFF = 1

Illumination is off.

docs.title: AUTO class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
AUTO = 2

Illumination mode is determined based on detector selection.

docs.title: GREYSCALE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
GREYSCALE = 3

Illumination is Infra-red, producing greyscale images.

docs.title: COLOR class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
COLOR = 4

Illumination is White light, producing color images.

docs.title: CommandId
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    framework required, not used for communication.

  • EXECUTE –

    The external procedure is currently executing.

  • STOP –

    The external procedure is about to stop it's execution.

  • PAUSE –

    The external procedure is paused from execution.

  • CONTINUE –

    The external procedure is continued from pause.

  • CLOSE –

    The external procedure is about to close .

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

framework required, not used for communication.

docs.title: EXECUTE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
EXECUTE = 1

The external procedure is currently executing.

docs.title: STOP class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
STOP = 2

The external procedure is about to stop it's execution.

docs.title: PAUSE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
PAUSE = 3

The external procedure is paused from execution.

docs.title: CONTINUE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
CONTINUE = 4

The external procedure is continued from pause.

docs.title: CLOSE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
CLOSE = 5

The external procedure is about to close .

docs.title: StageBacklashMode
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Stage backlash mode enumeration.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Unknown indicates that this variable hasn't been assigned yet.

  • DISABLED –

    Stage backlash is disabled.

  • POSITIVE –

    Stage backlash is enabled in the positive direction.

  • NEGATIVE –

    Stage backlash is enabled in the negative direction.

  • BOTH_DIRECTIONS_POSITIVE –

    Stage backlash is positive in both positive and negative directions.

  • BOTH_DIRECTIONS_NEGATIVE –

    Stage backlash is negative in both directions.

docs.title: NEGATIVE class-attribute instance-attribute

docs.title: BOTH_DIRECTIONS_POSITIVE class-attribute instance-attribute

docs.title: BOTH_DIRECTIONS_NEGATIVE class-attribute instance-attribute

)

Bases: Message

Response object of the camera live image acquisition.

Attributes:

  • result_type (AcquisitionResponseType) –

    A value indicating the type of response returned by the camera live image acquisition stream.

  • width (int) –

    The overall width of the image. This is only valid when the ResultType is FrameStart, otherwise it will be set to zero and not transmitted.

  • height (int) –

    The overall height of the image. This is only valid when the ResultType is FrameStart, otherwise it will be set to zero and not transmitted.

  • bytes_per_pixel (int) –

    The number of bytes per pixel. This is only valid when the ResultType is FrameStart, otherwise it will be set to zero and not transmitted.

  • total_image_size (int) –

    The total size of the image. This is only valid when the ResultType is FrameStart, otherwise it will be set to zero and not transmitted.

  • image_data (bytes) –

    The data buffer of this part of the image. This may be less than the total image size, in which case the client should expect more data to follow.

docs.title: ExtendedAcquisitionSettings dataclass
docs.text:
ExtendedAcquisitionSettings(
    detector_name: str = betterproto.string_field(1),
    width: int = betterproto.int32_field(2),
    height: int = betterproto.int32_field(3),
    dwell_time: float = betterproto.double_field(4),
    frame_averages: int = betterproto.int32_field(5),
    line_averages: int = betterproto.int32_field(6),
    scan_size_x: float = betterproto.double_field(7),
    scan_size_y: float = betterproto.double_field(8),
    scan_center_x: float = betterproto.double_field(9),
    scan_center_y: float = betterproto.double_field(10),
)

Bases: Message

Request object to set the simple acquisition settings.

Attributes:

  • detector_name (str) –

    The detector to use.

  • width (int) –

    The image width in pixels.

  • height (int) –

    The image height in pixels.

  • dwell_time (float) –

    The dwell time in seconds.

  • frame_averages (int) –

    The number of frames to average.

  • line_averages (int) –

    The number of lines to average.

  • scan_size_x (float) –

    The relative size of the scan field in x direction

  • scan_size_y (float) –

    The relative size of the scan field in y direction

  • scan_center_x (float) –

    The relative position of the scan field in x direction

  • scan_center_y (float) –

    The relative position of the scan field in y direction

docs.title: detector_name class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
detector_name: str = string_field(1)

The detector to use. Null will take the detector from the previous acquisition.

docs.title: width class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
width: int = int32_field(2)

The image width in pixels.

docs.title: height class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
height: int = int32_field(3)

The image height in pixels.

docs.title: dwell_time class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
dwell_time: float = double_field(4)

The dwell time in seconds. The closest dwell time to the selected one will be chosen.

docs.title: frame_averages class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
frame_averages: int = int32_field(5)

The number of frames to average. 1 means no averaging.

docs.title: line_averages class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
line_averages: int = int32_field(6)

docs.title: scan_size_x class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
scan_size_x: float = double_field(7)

The relative size of the scan field in x direction proportional to the full field of view, where the FOV equals 1. Range (0, 1].

docs.title: scan_size_y class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
scan_size_y: float = double_field(8)

The relative size of the scan field in y direction proportional to the full field of view, where the FOV equals 1. Range (0, 1].

docs.title: scan_center_x class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
scan_center_x: float = double_field(9)

The relative position of the scan field in x direction proportional to the full field of view, where the optical axis is at (0,0) and the FOV ranges from -0.5 to 0.5. Range (-0.5, 0.5).

docs.title: scan_center_y class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
scan_center_y: float = double_field(10)

The relative position of the scan field in y direction proportional to the full field of view, where the optical axis is at (0,0) and the FOV ranges from -0.5 to 0.5. Range (-0.5, 0.5).

docs.title: EdsAcquisitionMode
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Enum indicating the acquisition mode.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • AUTO –

    Auto selection of acquisition mode which is Counts.

  • LIVE_TIME –

    Acquire EDS data for a period of live time. This is the time for which the system is processing counts into the spectrum.

  • COUNTS –

    Acquisition continues until enough counts are collected in the spectrum.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: AUTO class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
AUTO = 1

Auto selection of acquisition mode which is Counts. Acquisition continues until enough counts are collected in the spectrum.

docs.title: LIVE_TIME class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
LIVE_TIME = 2

Acquire EDS data for a period of live time. This is the time for which the system is processing counts into the spectrum.

docs.title: COUNTS class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
COUNTS = 3

Acquisition continues until enough counts are collected in the spectrum.

docs.title: WorkflowState
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

WorkflowState.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default value.

  • RUN_ACTIVE –

    Execution running.

  • RUN_PAUSED –

    Execution paused.

  • RUN_COMPLETED –

    Execution completed.

  • RUN_FAILED –

    Execution failed.

  • RUN_UNKNOWN –

    Unknown status, e.g. no run so far.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default value.

docs.title: AxisIdentifier
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Unique identifier for axis.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • X –

    X axis (translation axis).

  • Y –

    Y axis (translation axis).

  • Z –

    Z axis (translation axis).

  • R –

    R axis (rotation axis).

  • T –

    T axis (rotation axis).

  • M –

    M axis (translation axis).

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: X class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
X = 1

X axis (translation axis). Controlled by length, default unit is meters.

docs.title: Y class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
Y = 2

Y axis (translation axis). Controlled by length, default unit is meters.

docs.title: Z class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
Z = 3

Z axis (translation axis). Controlled by length, default unit is meters.

docs.title: R class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
R = 4

R axis (rotation axis). Controlled by angle, default unit is radians.

docs.title: T class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
T = 5

T axis (rotation axis). Controlled by angle, default unit is radians.

docs.title: M class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
M = 6

M axis (translation axis). Controlled by length, default unit is meters.

docs.title: StageMotionState
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Stage motion state for ZenApi.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • UNKNOWN –

    Should not occur in a well configured system.

  • ERROR –

    The stage cannot perform any task. Software restart and/or physical intervention is required.

  • IDLE –

    The stage is not moving.

  • MOVING –

    The stage is in motion.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: UNKNOWN class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNKNOWN = 1

Should not occur in a well configured system.

docs.title: ERROR class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
ERROR = 2

The stage cannot perform any task. Software restart and/or physical intervention is required.

docs.title: IDLE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
IDLE = 3

The stage is not moving.

docs.title: MOVING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
MOVING = 4

The stage is in motion.

docs.title: StageState
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Stage state for ZenApi.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • UNKNOWN –

    Should not occur in a well configured system.

  • ERROR –

    The stage cannot perform any task. Software restart and/or

  • NORMAL –

    The state is functioning normally and can be used.

  • INITIALIZING –

    The stage is in the process of reinitializing one or more axes, should not be used, and will

  • INITIALIZATION_NEEDED –

    The stage will respond to motion commands, but positions may be reported erroneously,

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: UNKNOWN class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNKNOWN = 1

Should not occur in a well configured system.

docs.title: ERROR class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
ERROR = 2

The stage cannot perform any task. Software restart and/or physical intervention is required.

docs.title: NORMAL class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
NORMAL = 3

The state is functioning normally and can be used.

docs.title: INITIALIZING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
INITIALIZING = 4

The stage is in the process of reinitializing one or more axes, should not be used, and will ignore value sets and commands other than stop.

docs.title: INITIALIZATION_NEEDED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
INITIALIZATION_NEEDED = 5

The stage will respond to motion commands, but positions may be reported erroneously, and the stage should be initialized before further use.

docs.title: AutofocusContrastMeasure
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

The contrast measures of the software autofocus contrast mode.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • DEFAULT –

    The default contrast measure.

  • LOW_SIGNAL –

    The contrast measure to use in low-signal and calibration situations.

docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: DEFAULT class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
DEFAULT = 1

The default contrast measure.

docs.title: LOW_SIGNAL class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
LOW_SIGNAL = 2

The contrast measure to use in low-signal and calibration situations.

docs.title: AutofocusMode
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

The autofocus modes.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • CONTRAST –

    Sharpness is measured on the basis of contrast values.

  • INTENSITY –

    Sharpness is measured on the basis of intensity values.

  • AUTO –

    Automatic determination of measure method (Contrast or Intensity) in dependency of used hardware.

  • REFLEX –

    Sharpness is measured with the reflection mode autofocus.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: CONTRAST class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
CONTRAST = 1

Sharpness is measured on the basis of contrast values.

docs.title: INTENSITY class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
INTENSITY = 2

Sharpness is measured on the basis of intensity values.

docs.title: AUTO class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
AUTO = 3

Automatic determination of measure method (Contrast or Intensity) in dependency of used hardware.

docs.title: REFLEX class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
REFLEX = 4

Sharpness is measured with the reflection mode autofocus.

docs.title: AutofocusSampling
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Scales the depth of focus by predefined values given here.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • FINE –

    Do oversampling.

  • DEFAULT –

    Do sampling according to depth of focus.

  • MEDIUM –

    Do under sampling.

  • COARSE –

    Coarser than medium.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: FINE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
FINE = 1

Do oversampling.

docs.title: DEFAULT class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
DEFAULT = 2

Do sampling according to depth of focus.

docs.title: MEDIUM class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
MEDIUM = 3

Do under sampling.

docs.title: COARSE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
COARSE = 4

Coarser than medium.

docs.title: RegionEllipse dataclass
docs.text:
RegionEllipse(
    bounding_box: RegionRect = betterproto.message_field(1),
)

Bases: Message

Represents an elliptical region defined by a bounding rectangle.

Attributes:

  • bounding_box (RegionRect) –

    The rectangle that defines the bounding box of the ellipse.

docs.title: bounding_box class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
bounding_box: RegionRect = message_field(1)

The rectangle that defines the bounding box of the ellipse. The ellipse is fully contained within this rectangle. The ZenApi.LM.Acquisition.V1.RegionRect specifies the position and size of the bounding rectangle using four values: X (left), Y (top), Width, and Height. For example, a rectangle with X = 10, Y = 20, Width = 100, and Height = 50 defines an ellipse centered at (60, 45) with a horizontal radius of 50 and a vertical radius of 25.

docs.title: RegionPolygon dataclass
docs.text:
RegionPolygon(
    points: List[RegionPoint] = betterproto.message_field(
        1
    ),
)

Bases: Message

Represents a polygonal region defined by an ordered list of vertices.

Attributes:

  • points (List[RegionPoint]) –

    The ordered list of ZenApi.LM.Acquisition.V1.RegionPoint instances that define the vertices of the polygon.

docs.title: points class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
points: List[RegionPoint] = message_field(1)

The ordered list of ZenApi.LM.Acquisition.V1.RegionPoint instances that define the vertices of the polygon. Each point specifies an (X, Y) coordinate in the region's coordinate space. The polygon is formed by connecting each consecutive point in the list, and finally connecting the last point back to the first to close the shape.

Classes:

  • RegionPoint –

    Represents a two-dimensional point with X and Y coordinates for use in region-based acquisition scenarios.

  • RegionRect –

    Represents a rectangular region defined by its left (X), top (Y) coordinates, width, and height.

  • RegionContour –

    General data contract for all region contour types.

  • RegionEllipse –

    Represents an elliptical region defined by a bounding rectangle.

  • RegionPolygon –

    Represents a polygonal region defined by an ordered list of vertices.

  • RegionRectangle –

    Represents a rectangular region within the scan area.

docs.text:
RegionEllipse(
    bounding_box: RegionRect = betterproto.message_field(1),
)

Bases: Message

Represents an elliptical region defined by a bounding rectangle.

Attributes:

  • bounding_box (RegionRect) –

    The rectangle that defines the bounding box of the ellipse.

docs.title: bounding_box class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
bounding_box: RegionRect = message_field(1)

The rectangle that defines the bounding box of the ellipse. The ellipse is fully contained within this rectangle. The ZenApi.LM.Acquisition.V2.RegionRect specifies the position and size of the bounding rectangle using four values: X (left), Y (top), Width, and Height. For example, a rectangle with X = 10, Y = 20, Width = 100, and Height = 50 defines an ellipse centered at (60, 45) with a horizontal radius of 50 and a vertical radius of 25.

docs.title: RegionPolygon dataclass
docs.text:
RegionPolygon(
    points: List[RegionPoint] = betterproto.message_field(
        1
    ),
)

Bases: Message

Represents a polygonal region defined by an ordered list of vertices.

Attributes:

  • points (List[RegionPoint]) –

    The ordered list of ZenApi.LM.Acquisition.V2.RegionPoint instances that define the vertices of the polygon.

docs.title: points class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
points: List[RegionPoint] = message_field(1)

The ordered list of ZenApi.LM.Acquisition.V2.RegionPoint instances that define the vertices of the polygon. Each point specifies an (X, Y) coordinate in the region's coordinate space. The polygon is formed by connecting each consecutive point in the list, and finally connecting the last point back to the first to close the shape.

docs.title: zen_api.lm.slide_scan.v1
docs.text:
Classes:

  • SlideState –

    This enumeration names all possible slide processing states.

  • TraySlotState –

    Enum defining the states of slide scanner slot LEDs.

  • TrayType –

    Enum defining the types of slide scanner trays.

  • TrayWorkingState –

    Enum defining the types of slide scanner trays working state.

  • ResponseCode –

    Numerical result code.

  • ResponseType –

    Kind of failure with implication to error recovery.

  • ChannelSettings –

    Settings for a channel.

  • ProfileInformation –

    ProfileInformation contains information about the scan profile associated with a specific slide.

  • SlideInformation –

    SlideInformation holds information about an Axioscan slide.

  • TrayInformation –

    TrayInformation contains information about an Axioscan tray.

  • InformationBase –

    Base class for all information types.

  • MagazineInformation –

    Data container for inforamtion about the magazine state.

  • SimpleInformation –

    Data container for a simple message inforamtion.

  • SlideScanSystemInformation –

    Data container for information about the hardware state.

  • RegionDefinition –

    Represents definition of scan region.

  • ScanProfileOptionsOverride –

    Data container for overriding options of a scan profile.

  • SlidePositionInformation –

    SlidePositionInformation holds information about an AxioScan slide position.

  • GeneralResponse –

    A general response for all asynchronous requests that do not return any results or data.

  • SlideScanServiceGetChannelSettingsRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for retrieving the configured channels.

  • SlideScanServiceGetChannelSettingsResponse –

    Lists the configured channel settings in the specified scan profile.

  • SlideScanServiceGetMagazineStateRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for a call to retrieve the magazine state.

  • SlideScanServiceGetMagazineStateResponse –

    Lists the populated slides of each tray.

  • SlideScanServiceGetStorageFolderRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for a call to retrieve the storage folder for slide scans.

  • SlideScanServiceGetStorageFolderResponse –

    Describes the result for a call to retrieve the storage folder for slide scans.

  • SlideScanServiceObserveRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for a call to observe the events and progress

  • SlideScanServiceObserveResponse –

    Describes the output parameters for a call to observe the events, progress

  • SlideScanServiceResetSlideStatesRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for resetting the state of specified slides to new.

  • SlideScanServiceResetSlideStatesResponse –

    Describes the output parameters for resetting the state of specified slides.

  • SlideScanServiceSetStorageFolderRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for setting the storage folder for slide scans.

  • SlideScanServiceSetStorageFolderResponse –

    Describes the result for a call to set the storage folder.

  • SlideScanServiceStartScanPreviewRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for the preview start.

  • SlideScanServiceStartScanPreviewResponse –

    Response of starting a preview in the slide scan service.

  • SlideScanServiceStartScanProfileRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for the scan profile start.

  • SlideScanServiceStartScanProfileResponse –

    Describes the output parameters for a call to start the scan profile.

  • SlideScanServiceStopScanPreviewRequest –

    Represents a request to stop the preview in the SlideScan service.

  • SlideScanServiceStopScanPreviewResponse –

    Represents the response for stopping the preview in the slide scan service.

  • SlideScanServiceStopScanProfileRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for stopping the scan profile.

  • SlideScanServiceStopScanProfileResponse –

    Describes the output parameters for a call to stop the scan profile.

  • SlideScanServiceUnmarkSlidesRequest –

    Describes the input parameters for unmark slides.

  • SlideScanServiceUnmarkSlidesResponse –

    Describes the output parameters for a call to unmark slides.

docs.title: SlideState
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

This enumeration names all possible slide processing states.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default value if status is not specified.

  • STOPPED –

    Processing was stopped.

  • NEW –

    New (not processed).

  • PREVIEW_IN_PROGRESS –

    Preview currently in work.

  • INPUT_REQUIRED –

    Preview processing finished, but input required.

  • PREVIEWED –

    Preview processing finished.

  • SCAN_IN_PROGRESS –

    Scan currently in work.

  • FINISHED –

    Processing finished.

  • ERROR –

    Processing error occurred.

  • SKIPPED –

    Slide was skipped by user.

Default value if status is not specified.

Preview currently in work.

docs.text:
INPUT_REQUIRED = 4

Preview processing finished, but input required.

Preview processing finished.

docs.text:

docs.title: TraySlotState
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Enum defining the states of slide scanner slot LEDs.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default value if status is not specified.

  • SLOT_OPEN_SYSTEM_PAUSED –

    Slot swiveled out for assembling.

  • SLOT_OPEN_TRAY_LOADED –

    Slot swiveled out for assembling but Tray is on stage.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_TRAY_NOT_PROCESSED –

    Slot swiveled in with tray containing unprocessed slides.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_TRAY_PROCESSED –

    Slot swiveled in with tray containing processed slides.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_PROCESS_ERROR –

    Slot swiveled in with tray but an processing error was occurred.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_TRAY_PRESCANNED –

    Slot swiveled in with tray containing pre-scanned slides.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_TRAY_LOADED –

    Slot swiveled in and tray is on stage.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_NO_TRAY –

    Slot swiveled in without tray.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_TRAY_IN_SYSTEM_AND_SLOT –

    Slot swiveled in with tray and a tray on the stage (Error case).

  • SLOT_CLOSED_UNKNOWN_TRAY –

    Slot swiveled in with an unknown tray type.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_NO_SLIDES –

    Slot swiveled in with an empty tray.

  • SLOT_CLOSED_TRAY_PARTLY_PROCESSED_WITH_ERRORS –

    Slot swiveled in with tray containing processed slides.

  • UNKNOWN –

    Unknown type (tray is inserted but could not be determined).

docs.title: TrayType
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Enum defining the types of slide scanner trays.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default value if status is not specified.

  • NONE –

    No tray.

  • SCAN1X3 –

    Scan tray for 4 slides of size 1'x3'.

  • SCAN2X3 –

    Scan tray for 2 slides of size 2'x3'.

  • SCAN1X3_2X3 –

    Scan tray for 2 slides, one of size 1'x3', one of size 2'x3'.

  • CALIBRATION_STAGE –

    Stage calibration slide holder tray.

  • SCAN1X3_BASIC –

    Scan tray for 4 slides of size 1'x3', basic design.

  • SCAN4X3 –

    Scan tray for 1 slide of size 4'x3' or a combination of slides of unknown size.

  • PARKING –

    Arbitrary tray type to indicate the parking position. Is only available at parking position of the magazine changer.

  • UNKNOWN –

    Unknown type (tray is inserted but could not be determined).

docs.text:

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default value if the ResponseType is not specified.

  • SUCCESS –

    The API call is accepted and will be processed.

  • WARNING –

    The API call is accepted and will be processed.

  • FAILED –

    Something went wrong. This is the usual error type.

  • EXCEPTION –

    The call threw an exeption.

Bases: Message

TrayInformation contains information about an Axioscan tray.

Attributes:

  • position (int) –

    The position of the tray inside the Axioscan magazine in the range of [1, 26].

  • type (TrayType) –

    The ZenApi.LM.SlideScan.V1.TrayType of the tray. The type determines the number of possible slides on the tray.

  • working_state (TrayWorkingState) –

    The ZenApi.LM.SlideScan.V1.TrayWorkingState of the tray. The working state of the entire tray.

  • slot_state (TraySlotState) –

    The ZenApi.LM.SlideScan.V1.TraySlotState of the tray. The status of the tray slot ( open/closed statuses).

  • slides (List[SlideInformation]) –

    ZenApi.LM.SlideScan.V1.SlideInformation of the slides.

docs.text:
ScanProfileOptionsOverride(
    make_prescan_preview: Optional[
        bool
    ] = betterproto.message_field(
        1, wraps=(betterproto.TYPE_BOOL)
    ),
    use_manual_segmentation: Optional[
        bool
    ] = betterproto.message_field(
        2, wraps=(betterproto.TYPE_BOOL)
    ),
    disable_label_detection: Optional[
        bool
    ] = betterproto.message_field(
        3, wraps=(betterproto.TYPE_BOOL)
    ),
)

Bases: Message

Data container for overriding options of a scan profile.

Attributes:

  • make_prescan_preview (Optional[bool]) –

    A value indicating whether the prescan preview should be made or only a preview with the preview cam.

  • use_manual_segmentation (Optional[bool]) –

    A value indicating whether the segmentation mode shall be manual.

  • disable_label_detection (Optional[bool]) –

    A value indicating whether the label detection shall be disabled.

docs.title: make_prescan_preview class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
make_prescan_preview: Optional[bool] = message_field(
    1, wraps=TYPE_BOOL
)

A value indicating whether the prescan preview should be made or only a preview with the preview cam. If set to perform a prescan, the prescan will be done according to existing settings in the scan profile without modifications (resp. default settings will be used).

docs.title: use_manual_segmentation class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
use_manual_segmentation: Optional[bool] = message_field(
    2, wraps=TYPE_BOOL
)

A value indicating whether the segmentation mode shall be manual. If not set or set to false, the segmentation will be performed as defined in the scan profile.

docs.title: disable_label_detection class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
disable_label_detection: Optional[bool] = message_field(
    3, wraps=TYPE_BOOL
)

A value indicating whether the label detection shall be disabled. If set to true, the barcode and ocr recognition will be disabled. If not set or set to false, the label detection will be performed as defined in the scan profile.

Classes:

docs.title: StartJobOptions dataclass
docs.text:
StartJobOptions(
    result_path: str = betterproto.string_field(1),
)

Bases: Message

Start Job options.

Attributes:

  • result_path (str) –

    A value indicating a path for saving a Job results in the file system.

docs.title: result_path class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
result_path: str = string_field(1)

A value indicating a path for saving a Job results in the file system. -- If value is given than Job output will be copied to given path in filesystem and not uploaded to ZEN Archive. Have to be in the Windows-supported directory path format (local drive or network share). -- If value is null (or empty/whitespace) than Job output will be uploaded to ZEN Archive and not copied to anywhere.

docs.title: zen_api.workflows.v2
docs.text:
Classes:

  • JobStatus –

    Runtime status of the Job.

  • JobInfo –

    Information about Job executed by the workflow runner contains a real-time updates and Job's events with Job state.

  • WorkflowServiceGetJobInfoRequest –

    The WorkflowServiceGetJobInfoRequest class.

  • WorkflowServiceGetJobInfoResponse –

    Response object representing the job info.

  • WorkflowServiceStartJobRequest –

    The WorkflowServiceStartJobRequest class.

  • WorkflowServiceStartJobResponse –

    Response object representing the starting of a job.

  • WorkflowServiceStopJobRequest –

    The WorkflowServiceStopJobRequest class.

  • WorkflowServiceStopJobResponse –

    The WorkflowServiceStopJobResponse class.

  • WorkflowServiceWaitJobRequest –

    The WorkflowServiceWaitJobRequest class.

  • WorkflowServiceWaitJobResponse –

    The WorkflowServiceWaitJobResponse class.

docs.title: JobStatus
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Runtime status of the Job.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default value if status is not specified.

  • RUNNING –

    Job is currently executed by the workflow runner.

  • PAUSED –

    Job execution is paused (not supported for now and reserved only for future purposes

  • COMPLETED –

    Job execution is successfully completed.

  • FAILED –

    Job execution was failed.

  • CANCELLED –

    Job execution was interrupted (cancelled).

  • PENDING –

    Job is created but still not started and waiting for beginning of execution.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default value if status is not specified.

docs.title: RUNNING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
RUNNING = 1

Job is currently executed by the workflow runner.

docs.title: PAUSED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
PAUSED = 2

Job execution is paused (not supported for now and reserved only for future purposes for saving numeration sequence).

docs.title: COMPLETED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
COMPLETED = 3

Job execution is successfully completed.

docs.title: FAILED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
FAILED = 4

Job execution was failed.

docs.title: CANCELLED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
CANCELLED = 5

Job execution was interrupted (cancelled).

docs.title: PENDING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
PENDING = 6

Job is created but still not started and waiting for beginning of execution.

docs.title: JobStatus
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Enumerates possible job statuses.

Attributes:

  • UNSPECIFIED –

    Default enum value.

  • NOT_LOADED –

    Job template is not loaded.

  • LOADED –

    Job template is loaded.

  • RUNNING –

    Job is running.

  • FINALIZING –

    Finalizing job results after the job was completed successfully.

  • ARCHIVING –

    Archiving job results after they were finalized.

  • COMPLETED –

    Job was completed successfully.

  • ABORTED –

    Job was aborted.

  • CANCELED –

    Job was canceled.

docs.title: UNSPECIFIED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
UNSPECIFIED = 0

Default enum value.

docs.title: NOT_LOADED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
NOT_LOADED = 1

Job template is not loaded.

docs.title: LOADED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
LOADED = 2

Job template is loaded.

docs.title: RUNNING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
RUNNING = 3

Job is running.

docs.title: FINALIZING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
FINALIZING = 4

Finalizing job results after the job was completed successfully.

docs.title: ARCHIVING class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
ARCHIVING = 5

Archiving job results after they were finalized.

docs.title: COMPLETED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
COMPLETED = 6

Job was completed successfully.

docs.title: ABORTED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
ABORTED = 7

Job was aborted.

docs.title: CANCELED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
CANCELED = 8

Job was canceled.
